diseaseName: Alergia à Proteína do Leite de Vaca (APLV)

prevalence.population: Infants in the first year of life
prevalence.source: Pediatric allergy epidemiology syntheses; Brazilian pediatric allergy practice patterns

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant caregiver burden and nutrition impacts
burden.mortalityRate: Very low; severe anaphylaxis uncommon but possible

vulnerablePopulations: families with limited access to allergists and dietitians
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical history of immediate (IgE-mediated) or delayed symptoms; elimination diet with controlled re-challenge; specific IgE/skin tests when IgE-mediated suspected; rule out non-allergic intolerance

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands of infants annually
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Good with strict avoidance and appropriate formula; variable due to formula access and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Pediatric evaluation; oral food challenge capacity varies and is concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Elimination of cow’s milk protein; extensively hydrolyzed formula (eHF) or amino-acid formula (AAF) for severe cases; nutrition counseling; periodic supervised tolerance assessment
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Cost and availability of specialized formulas; overdiagnosis/underdiagnosis in primary care; limited allergy services outside capitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective management when formulas and counseling are accessible; increasing awareness in pediatrics
